onset.Description: Event onset time in seconds from the beginning of the EEG recording.

duration.Description: Event duration in seconds (0 if unknown).

value.Description: BrainVision trigger codes as recorded in EEG.event(x).type.

Levels.S 11: Eyes OPEN: start phase 1 marker (task-specific segmentation marker; open condition is assumed to be active from recording start until the first eyes-closed marker).
Levels.S 12: Eyes OPEN: start phase 2 marker (if multiple open blocks occur).
Levels.S 13: Eyes OPEN: start phase 3 marker (if multiple open blocks occur).
Levels.S 14: Eyes OPEN: start phase 4 marker (if multiple open blocks occur).
Levels.S 21: Eyes CLOSED: start phase 1 marker (closed condition begins at the first marker starting with '2', typically S 21).
Levels.S 22: Eyes CLOSED: start phase 2 marker (if multiple closed blocks occur).
Levels.S 23: Eyes CLOSED: start phase 3 marker (if multiple closed blocks occur).
Levels.S 24: Eyes CLOSED: start phase 4 marker (if multiple closed blocks occur).
Levels.S 99: End of baseline block (marks the end of the last eyes-closed block; any data after S 99 is treated as Eyes OPEN again).
Levels.boundary: EEGLAB boundary event indicating a recording discontinuity (pause, cut, or concatenation).

SegmentationPolicy.Description: Segmentation into Eyes OPEN vs Eyes CLOSED uses trigger families S 1x (open markers) and S 2x (closed markers) plus S 99 as the baseline end marker.
Rules: Eyes OPEN starts at the beginning of the recording (t=0), not at S 11.
The first Eyes CLOSED block starts at the first trigger whose code starts with '2' (typically S 21).
Eyes CLOSED ends at S 99 (end of baseline).
Any data after S 99 is treated as Eyes OPEN again.
Within each OPEN/CLOSED chunk, fixed-length 10 s epochs are created using eeg_regepochs (as many non-overlapping epochs as fit).
